en.extract:
The Senegal eremomela (Eremomela pusilla) is a member of the  African warbler family, the Cisticolidae. It occurs in the savannas of western Sub-Saharan Africa.


Taxonomy
The Senegal eremomela was described by the German ornithologist Gustav Hartlaub in 1857 and given its current binomial name Eremomela pusilla. The type locality is Senegal. The species has been treated as conspecific with the green-backed eremomela, as they hybridizes in a narrow zone of sympatry along the Cameroon-Central African Republic border.


Description
The Senegal eremomela is a small bird with a pale brownish grey head and a pale white supercilium. The back and mantle are green, becoming a brighter yellowish green on the rump, the throat and upper breast are white becoming bright lemon yellow on the lower breast, belly and vent. Blackish bill with a pale lower mandible and pale brownish legs.


Voice
The territorial call is a rarely heard harsh and rapid chattering. It trills constantly when foraging. It joins the morning chorus with a dull monotonous and rhythmic "whup-whup-whup" sand from a perch before and at the start of the breeding season.


Distribution
Western Africa from extreme southern Mauritania and Senegal to north western Cameroon, south-western Chad and far north-western Central African Republic.


Habits and habitat
The Senegal eremomela occurs in small parties which actively forage in wooded savanna, cultivated areas and orchards. It is arboreal and insectivorous feeding on ants, beetles, caterpillars and other larvae, as well as some fruit. It breeds in the first half of the year in the west, although egg laying has been recorded as late as October in the Ivory Coast.


References

Birds of The Gambia by Barlow, Wacher and Disley, ISBN 1-873403-32-1


External links
Recordings of the Senegal Eremomela at Xeno-Canto

The '''Senegal eremomela''' ('''''Eremomela pusilla''''') is a member of the  African warbler family, the [[Cisticolidae]]. It occurs in the savannas of western [[Sub-Saharan Africa]].

==Taxonomy==
The Senegal eremomela was [[Species description|described]] by the German ornithologist [[Gustav Hartlaub]] in 1857 and given its current [[binomial name]] ''Eremomela pusilla''. The [[Type locality (biology)|type locality]] is Senegal.<ref>{{cite book | last=Hartlaub | first=Gustav | author-link=Gustav Hartlaub | year=1857 | title=System der Ornithologie Westafrica's | publisher=C. Schünemann | place=Bremen | page=59 | url=https://www.biodiversitylibrary.org/page/48048882 }}</ref><ref>{{cite book | editor1-last=Mayr | editor1-first=Ernst | editor1-link=Ernst Mayr | editor2-last=Cottrell | editor2-first=G. William | year=1986 | title=Check-list of Birds of the World | volume=11 | publisher=Museum of Comparative Zoology | place=Cambridge, Massachusetts | page=202 | url=https://www.biodiversitylibrary.org/page/14483903 }}</ref> The species has been treated as [[conspecific]] with the [[green-backed eremomela]], as they hybridizes in a narrow zone of [[sympatry]] along the Cameroon-Central African Republic border.<ref name = HBW/>

==Description==
The Senegal eremomela is a small bird with a pale brownish grey head and a pale white supercilium. The back and mantle are green, becoming a brighter yellowish green on the rump, the throat and upper breast are white becoming bright lemon yellow on the lower breast, belly and vent. Blackish bill with a pale lower mandible and pale brownish legs.<ref name = B&D>{{cite book | first1 = Nik | last1 = Borrow | first2 = Ron | last2 = Demey | year = 2001 | title = Birds of Western Africa | publisher = A & C Black | page = 645 | isbn = 0-7136-3959-8}}</ref>

[[File:Ornithologie Nordost-Afrika's - der Nilquellen- und Küsten Gebiete des Rothen Meeres und des nördlichen Somal-Landes (1869) (14747524201).jpg|thumb|right]]

===Voice===
The territorial call is a rarely heard harsh and rapid chattering.<ref name = B&D/> It trills constantly when foraging. It joins the morning chorus with a dull monotonous and rhythmic "''whup-whup-whup''" sand from a perch before and at the start of the breeding season.<ref name = B&D/>

==Distribution==
Western Africa from extreme southern [[Mauritania]] and [[Senegal]] to north western [[Cameroon]], south-western [[Chad]] and far north-western [[Central African Republic]].<ref name = HBW>{{cite web | last=Pearson | first=D. | year=2017 | title=Senegal Eremomela (''Eremomela pusilla'') | editor1-last=del Hoyo | editor1-first=J. | editor2-last=Elliott | editor2-first=A. | editor3-last=Sargatal | editor3-first=J. | editor4-last=Christie | editor4-first=D.A. | editor5-last=de Juana | editor5-first=E. | work=Handbook of the Birds of the World Alive | publisher=Lynx Edicions | url=http://www.hbw.com/node/58930 | access-date=27 August 2017 |url-access=subscription }}</ref>

==Habits and habitat==
The Senegal eremomela occurs in small parties which actively forage in wooded savanna, cultivated areas and orchards. It is arboreal and insectivorous feeding on ants, beetles, caterpillars and other larvae, as well as some fruit. It breeds in the first half of the year in the west, although egg laying has been recorded as late as October in the [[Ivory Coast]].<ref name= B&D/><ref name = HBW/>

==References==
{{Reflist}}
* ''Birds of The Gambia'' by Barlow, Wacher and Disley, {{ISBN|1-873403-32-1}}

==External links==
